primaryStudies.citation: Szeto HH. First-in-class cardiolipin-protective compound as a therapeutic agent to restore mitochondrial bioenergetics. Br J Pharmacol. 2014;171(8):2029-2050.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of SS-31 mechanism - binds cardiolipin on inner mitochondrial membrane, restores electron transport chain function, reduces ROS production without scavenging

primaryStudies.citation: Birk AV, Liu S, Soong Y, et al. The mitochondrial-targeted compound SS-31 re-energizes ischemic mitochondria by interacting with cardiolipin. J Am Soc Nephrol. 2013;24(8):1250-1261.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: SS-31 binds selectively to cardiolipin, optimizes cytochrome c function, prevents cardiolipin peroxidation during ischemia-reperfusion injury

primaryStudies.citation: Sabbah HN, Gupta RC, Kohli S, et al. Chronic Therapy With Elamipretide (MTP-131), a Novel Mitochondria-Targeting Peptide, Improves Left Ventricular and Mitochondrial Function in Dogs With Advanced Heart Failure. Circ Heart Fail. 2016;9(2):e002206.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: 3-month treatment improved LVEF, reduced LV volumes, normalized mitochondrial respiration, reduced ROS; first preclinical evidence in advanced HF

primaryStudies.citation: Butler J, Khan MS, Anker SD, et al. Effects of Elamipretide on Left Ventricular Function in Patients With Heart Failure With Reduced Ejection Fraction: The PROGRESS-HF Phase 2 Trial. J Card Fail. 2020;26(5):429-437.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Primary endpoint (LVESV change) not met; secondary endpoints showed trends in LVEF improvement; well tolerated; underpowered study

primaryStudies.citation: Karaa A, Haas R, Goldstein A, et al. Randomized dose-escalation trial of elamipretide in adults with primary mitochondrial myopathy. Neurology. 2018;90(14):e1212-e1221.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Dose-dependent improvement in 6-minute walk test distance; acceptable safety profile; supported Phase 3 development

primaryStudies.citation: Karaa A, Goldstein A, Engel WK, et al. MMPOWER-3: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial of elamipretide in primary mitochondrial myopathy. Neurology. 2023;101(3):e238-e252.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Primary endpoint (6MWT) not met at 24 weeks; post-hoc analyses showed improvements in subgroups; trial continued to 144 weeks; FDA declined approval

primaryStudies.citation: Reid Thompson W, Hornby B, Manuel R, et al. A phase 2/3 randomized clinical trial followed by an open-label extension to evaluate the effectiveness of elamipretide in Barth syndrome. Genet Med. 2021;23(2):269-277.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Improved 6MWT, muscle strength, and patient-reported fatigue; excellent safety; supported by mechanism (Barth = cardiolipin deficiency)

primaryStudies.citation: Daubert MA, Yow E, Duber G, et al. Novel Mitochondria-Targeting Peptide in Heart Failure Treatment: A Randomized, Placebo-Controlled Trial of Elamipretide. Circ Heart Fail. 2017;10(12):e004389.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Single IV infusion well tolerated; dose-dependent reduction in LVESV at 4 hours; proof-of-concept for acute cardiac effects

primaryStudies.citation: Siegel MP, Kruse SE, Percival JM, et al. Mitochondrial-targeted peptide rapidly improves mitochondrial energetics and skeletal muscle performance in aged mice. Aging Cell. 2013;12(5):763-771.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Single hour treatment reversed age-related mitochondrial dysfunction in skeletal muscle; improved fatigue resistance; rapid onset of action

primaryStudies.citation: Zhao K, Zhao GM, Wu D, et al. Cell-permeable peptide antioxidants targeted to inner mitochondrial membrane inhibit mitochondrial swelling, oxidative cell death, and reperfusion injury. J Biol Chem. 2004;279(33):34682-34690.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Original characterization of SS peptides; SS-31 concentrated 1000-5000x in mitochondria; inhibits mPTP opening and cytochrome c release

primaryStudies.citation: Eirin A, Ebrahimi B, Zhang X, et al. Mitochondrial protection restores renal function in swine atherosclerotic renovascular disease. Cardiovasc Res. 2014;103(4):461-472.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: 4-week SS-31 treatment restored renal blood flow, GFR, and oxygenation; reduced fibrosis; preserved mitochondrial density

primaryStudies.citation: Yang L, Zhao K, Calingasan NY, et al. Mitochondria targeted peptides protect against 1-methyl-4-phenyl-1,2,3,6-tetrahydropyridine neurotoxicity. Antioxid Redox Signal. 2009;11(9):2095-2104.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: SS-31 prevented dopaminergic neuron loss and motor deficits; protected against mitochondrial complex I inhibition

primaryStudies.citation: Mitchell W, Ng EA, Taber R, et al. The mitochondria-targeted peptide SS-31 binds lipid bilayers and modulates surface electrostatics as a key component of its mechanism of action. J Biol Chem. 2020;295(21):7452-7469.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: SS-31 partitions into lipid bilayers, modulates membrane electrostatics; provides biophysical basis for cardiolipin interaction

primaryStudies.citation: Brown DA, Hale SL, Baines CP, et al. Reduction of early reperfusion injury with the mitochondria-targeting peptide Bendavia. J Cardiovasc Pharmacol Ther. 2014;19(1):121-132.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Bendavia reduced infarct size 20-35% when given at reperfusion; protected mitochondrial function; did not affect hemodynamics

primaryStudies.citation: Gibson CM, Giugliano RP, Kloner RA, et al. EMBRACE STEMI study: a Phase 2a trial to evaluate the safety, tolerability, and efficacy of intravenous MTP-131 on reperfusion injury in patients undergoing primary percutaneous coronary intervention. Eur Heart J. 2016;37(16):1296-1303.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Primary endpoint (CK-MB AUC) not significant; well tolerated; trends in infarct size reduction in anterior MI subgroup

primaryStudies.citation: Chatfield KC, Sparagna GC, Chau S, et al. Elamipretide Improves Mitochondrial Function in the Failing Human Heart. JACC Basic Transl Sci. 2019;4(2):147-157.
primaryStudies.population: Human failing heart tissue from transplant patients
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Elamipretide improved mitochondrial respiration in failing human heart tissue; restored cardiolipin content; first human tissue evidence

primaryStudies.citation: Stealth BioTherapeutics. Stealth BioTherapeutics Reports Topline Results from TAZPOWER Primary Mitochondrial Myopathy Phase 3 Trial. Press Release. 2021.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: TAZPOWER Phase 3 did not meet primary endpoint; FDA Complete Response Letter issued; company continues long-term extension studies

primaryStudies.citation: Grosser JA, Fehrman RL, Keefe KA, et al. The effects of a mitochondrial targeted peptide (elamipretide/SS31) on BAX recruitment and activation during apoptosis. BMC Res Notes. 2021;14(1):198.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: SS-31 prevents BAX oligomerization at mitochondrial membrane; reduces cytochrome c release; anti-apoptotic mechanism independent of ROS scavenging

europePMCStudies.citation: Petri S, Kiaei M, Damiano M, et al. Cell-permeable peptide antioxidants as a novel therapeutic approach in a mouse model of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis. J Neurochem. 2006;98(4):1141-1148.
europePMCStudies.keyFindings: SS-31 extended survival, improved motor function, reduced oxidative damage; first neurodegenerative disease model

europePMCStudies.citation: Dai DF, Chen T, Szeto H, et al. Mitochondrial targeted antioxidant peptide ameliorates hypertensive cardiomyopathy. J Am Coll Cardiol. 2011;58(1):73-82.
europePMCStudies.keyFindings: SS-31 prevented cardiac hypertrophy and fibrosis; improved diastolic function; reduced mitochondrial ROS; preserved cardiolipin

europePMCStudies.citation: Toyama S, Shimoyama N, Szeto HH, et al. Protective effect of a mitochondria-targeted peptide against the development of chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy in mice. ACS Chem Neurosci. 2018;9(7):1566-1571.
europePMCStudies.keyFindings: SS-31 prevented mechanical allodynia; protected mitochondrial function in dorsal root ganglia; potential for CIPN prevention

europePMCStudies.citation: Calkins MJ, Manczak M, Mao P, et al. Impaired mitochondrial biogenesis, defective axonal transport of mitochondria, abnormal mitochondrial dynamics and synaptic degeneration in a mouse model of Alzheimer's disease. Hum Mol Genet. 2012;21(20):4515-4529.
europePMCStudies.keyFindings: SS-31 restored mitochondrial transport, improved synaptic viability; provided rationale for AD therapeutic development

europePMCStudies.citation: Saad A, Herrmann SMS, Eirin A, et al. Phase 2a Clinical Trial of Mitochondrial Protection (Elamipretide) During Stent Revascularization in Patients With Atherosclerotic Renal Artery Stenosis. Circ Cardiovasc Interv. 2017;10(9):e005487.
europePMCStudies.population: 14 patients with atherosclerotic renal artery stenosis
europePMCStudies.keyFindings: Elamipretide during stenting improved cortical perfusion and reduced inflammatory markers; proof-of-concept for renal protection

stealthBioTrials.citation: NCT02805790 - TAZPOWER: Phase 3 Study of Elamipretide in Primary Mitochondrial Myopathy
stealthBioTrials.keyFindings: Primary endpoint not met (6MWT at 24 weeks); long-term extension showed durable improvements at 144 weeks in completers

stealthBioTrials.citation: NCT03323749 - SPIMM-301: Phase 3 Study of Elamipretide in Primary Mitochondrial Myopathy (subcutaneous)
stealthBioTrials.keyFindings: Subcutaneous formulation study; primary endpoint not met

stealthBioTrials.citation: NCT03098797 - ReCLAIM: Phase 2 Study in Age-Related Macular Degeneration
stealthBioTrials.keyFindings: Subcutaneous elamipretide improved low-luminance visual acuity; supported further development in geographic atrophy

stealthBioTrials.citation: NCT03891875 - ReCLAIM-2: Phase 2b Study in Dry AMD with Geographic Atrophy
stealthBioTrials.keyFindings: Evaluating effect on geographic atrophy progression

stealthBioTrials.citation: NCT02367014 - Phase 2 Trial in Barth Syndrome (TAZPOWER-Barth)
stealthBioTrials.keyFindings: Significant improvements in 6MWT (+67m), muscle strength, fatigue scores; mechanistically rational given cardiolipin abnormality in Barth

stealthBioTrials.citation: NCT02388464 - EMBRACE STEMI: Phase 2a in ST-Elevation Myocardial Infarction
stealthBioTrials.keyFindings: Primary endpoint (CK-MB AUC) not met; acceptable safety; exploratory signals in anterior MI

stealthBioTrials.citation: NCT02814097 - PROGRESS-HF: Phase 2 in Heart Failure with Reduced Ejection Fraction
stealthBioTrials.keyFindings: Primary endpoint (LVESV) not met; trends in LVEF improvement; well tolerated

stealthBioTrials.citation: NCT02245620 - Phase 1/2 in Primary Mitochondrial Myopathy
stealthBioTrials.keyFindings: Dose-dependent 6MWT improvement; acceptable safety; supported Phase 3 development

globalMitochondrialResearch.citation: Min K, Smuder AJ, Kwon OS, et al. Mitochondrial-targeted antioxidants protect skeletal muscle against immobilization-induced muscle atrophy. J Appl Physiol. 2011;111(5):1459-1466.
globalMitochondrialResearch.keyFindings: SS-31 attenuated immobilization-induced muscle atrophy by 30%; protected mitochondrial function; reduced proteolysis

globalMitochondrialResearch.citation: Powers SK, Hudson MB, Nelson WB, et al. Mitochondria-targeted antioxidants protect against mechanical ventilation-induced diaphragm weakness. Crit Care Med. 2011;39(7):1749-1759.
globalMitochondrialResearch.keyFindings: SS-31 prevented ventilator-induced diaphragm dysfunction; protected contractile function; reduced oxidative stress

globalMitochondrialResearch.citation: Anderson EJ, Lustig ME, Boyle KE, et al. Mitochondrial H2O2 emission and cellular redox state link excess fat intake to insulin resistance in both rodents and humans. J Clin Invest. 2009;119(3):573-581.
globalMitochondrialResearch.population: Rodent models and human muscle biopsies
globalMitochondrialResearch.keyFindings: SS-31 prevented high-fat diet-induced insulin resistance; demonstrated mitochondrial ROS link to metabolic disease

globalMitochondrialResearch.citation: Kloner RA, Hale SL, Dai W, et al. Reduction of ischemia/reperfusion injury with Bendavia, a mitochondria-targeting cytoprotective peptide. J Am Heart Assoc. 2012;1(3):e001644.
globalMitochondrialResearch.keyFindings: Bendavia reduced infarct size by 18-29% across multiple endpoints; effective when given at reperfusion

globalMitochondrialResearch.citation: Mouchiroud L, Houtkooper RH, Moullan N, et al. The NAD(+)/Sirtuin Pathway Modulates Longevity through Activation of Mitochondrial UPR and FOXO Signaling. Cell. 2013;154(2):430-441.
globalMitochondrialResearch.keyFindings: Context for mitochondrial-targeted therapies in aging; SS-31 mentioned as comparison compound for mitochondrial protection strategies

trials.title: TAZPOWER: Efficacy and Safety of Elamipretide in Subjects With Primary Mitochondrial Myopathy
trials.notes: Primary endpoint not met; FDA CRL issued

trials.title: SPIMM-301: Subcutaneous Elamipretide in Primary Mitochondrial Myopathy
ReCLAIM-2: Elamipretide in Geographic Atrophy Secondary to Dry AMD
ReCLAIM: Elamipretide Topline in Dry AMD
PROGRESS-HF: Elamipretide in Heart Failure With Reduced Ejection Fraction
conditions: Heart Failure With Reduced Ejection Fraction
trials.notes: Primary endpoint not met; trends noted

trials.title: EMBRACE STEMI: Elamipretide in ST-Elevation Myocardial Infarction
trials.notes: Primary endpoint not met; safety confirmed

trials.title: Phase 1/2 Dose-Escalation Study in Primary Mitochondrial Myopathy
Elamipretide in Leber's Hereditary Optic Neuropathy (LHON)

evidenceSummary.regulatoryStatus: Not FDA approved; multiple Phase 2/3 trials completed; FDA CRL for PMM indication; ongoing development in AMD/geographic atrophy
evidenceSummary.keyLimitations: Multiple Phase 3 trials did not meet primary endpoints despite strong preclinical rationale; Barth syndrome showed positive results in small trial; debate ongoing about optimal patient selection and endpoints
